extraction.one_line_summary: EWAN Caleb won by sitting on ACKERMANN Pascal's wheel until 450m to go, timing his sprint perfectly in crosswind finale.
tactical_patterns: Bora-hansgrohe denied ACKERMANN sprint points at intermediate by SELIG outsprinting, revealing team friction over ciclamino competition.
EWAN deliberately chose ACKERMANN's wheel over others because Pascal already proven fastest with best train, tactical wheel choice over team loyalty.
Dimension Data positioned NIZZOLO in final kilometer but lacked finishing speed, highlighting leadout execution without firepower problem.
Crosswind finale at 1km forced teams left, creating positioning chaos that benefited wheel-surfers over organized trains.

rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: positive: admitted being more relaxed after first win saved energy, suggests psychological burden of winless GT affects sprint performance
rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: negative: went too early at 450m despite having fastest leadout, timing error cost certain victory against weaker finisher
rider_signals.VIVIANI Elia: negative: stuck to predetermined plan rather than adapting to positioning chaos, inflexible tactics continued winless streak
rider_signals.NIZZOLO Giacomo: negative: perfect leadout positioning but lacks raw speed to convert, perennial bridesmaid without stage wins despite two points jerseys
rider_signals.DIMA Damiano: positive: actively targeting ciclamino with 700km+ breakaway kilometers, strategic sprint point accumulation through breakaway monopoly
